meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Template to Break Through Coding Plateaus
content.seo_description: Break free from basic app tutorials. Use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to master intermediate-level development and build your portfolio.
content.answer_block: You are stuck because you are building tutorials, not products. The Antigravity project template provides a production-grade architecture that forces you to handle authentication, state management, and API integration. By leveraging Claude Code to scaffold these complex patterns, you shift from a coding student to a software engineer.
content.intro:
The screen glows with the same boilerplate code you've written a dozen times. You understand the syntax, but the gap between 'Hello World' and a functional, scalable SaaS application feels like a chasm you can't cross. You are caught in the tutorial loop, building apps that look like everyone else's and disappear the moment you close your laptop.

But professional software isn't about writing more lines; it's about mastering architectural patterns. The Antigravity project template is designed to force you out of your comfort zone by introducing real-world complexity—modular routing, secure middleware, and robust database schemas—that transforms your portfolio from a collection of exercises into a showcase of engineering maturity.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish a professional project foundation that includes built-in CI/CD pipelines and type-safe environment configurations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a new project using the Antigravity standard template with TypeScript, Supabase authentication, and Tailwind CSS modules.

steps.title: Define Domain-Driven Models
steps.description: Move beyond simple arrays by implementing structured database schemas that mirror real business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema for a project management tool including Users, Workspaces, and Tasks with proper relational mapping.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate prisma schema based on workspace entity'

steps.title: Implement Middleware Security
steps.description: Secure your application routes by handling session validation at the edge, a key skill for intermediate developers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Antigravity middleware to protect /dashboard routes and redirect unauthenticated users to the login page.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply auth middleware to all routes under /dashboard'

steps.title: Integrate API Service Layer
steps.description: Decouple your frontend from data fetching logic to ensure your code is maintainable and testable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a centralized API service layer in Antigravity to handle CRUD operations for tasks using React Query.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor data fetching into a dedicated hooks folder'

steps.title: Deploy to Production Infrastructure
steps.description: Bridge the gap between local development and the real world by deploying your app to a live environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment configuration for Vercel that includes environment variable validation and build-time optimization.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure deployment pipeline for production'

pro_tips: Always use strict TypeScript modes; it forces you to understand the shape of your data, which is the #1 trait of a senior developer.
Never hardcode environment variables; use the Antigravity .env.example pattern to ensure team compatibility.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your utility functions before you write the UI; it prevents 'feature bloat' and bugs.
Modularize your CSS by component rather than by page to avoid the 'global styles' nightmare.
Audit your bundle size early; if your initial load exceeds 200kb, you are likely importing too many heavy libraries.
content.next_steps: Once your app is deployed, focus on adding a 'Pro' feature like CSV exports or user-specific analytics to differentiate your portfolio from standard bootcamps.
content.conversion_block: You are closer to launching your first production-grade app than you think. Stop building for practice, start building for impact. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
